{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Content Creator",
  "description": "Above Lifestyle is a premium service restaurant, lounge and club located in Lekki, Ikotun and Abuja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creator

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Duties and Responsibilities


	Content Creators are responsible for designing and generating engaging content in various formats, which can include written articles, videos, social media posts, and podcasts.
	They craft content that is tailored to appeal to specific audiences and meets the objectives of the organization or client.


The duties and responsibilities of a Content Creator include:


	Researching industry-related topics to create informative and appealing content
	Collaborating with marketing and design teams to plan and develop site content, style and layout
	Writing, editing, and proofreading content to ensure it is free of errors and maximizes SEO
	Creating engaging content for social media platforms that aligns with brand voice and marketing goals
	Coordinating with team members to create innovative content ideas
	Monitoring feedback and online conversations about the brand or products to inform content creation
	Keeping up-to-date with industry trends and popular content strategies
	Measuring the success of content using analytics tools and adjusting strategies based on the data.
	Creatingengaging and on-brand content for a variety of platforms from conception through to delivery.


Requirements and Skills


	Proven work experience as a Content Creator,or similar rolein the hospitality industry
	Portfolio of published articles
	Hands-on experience with Content Management Systems (e.g. WordPress)
	Excellent writing and editing skills in English
	An ability to fact-check long-form content pieces
	Time-management skills
	Detail-oriented content creator
	Ability to produce high-quality content for our business.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@abovelifestyle.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",

Content Creator Job at Above Lifestyle




        
    

        
        Above Lifestyle is a premium service restaurant, lounge and club located in Lekki, Ikotun and Abuja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creator

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Duties and Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Content Creators are responsible for designing and generating engaging content in various formats, which can include written articles, videos, social media posts, and podcasts.
	They craft content that is tailored to appeal to specific audiences and meets the objectives of the organization or client.


The duties and responsibilities of a Content Creator include:


	Researching industry-related topics to create informative and appealing content
	Collaborating with marketing and design teams to plan and develop site content, style and layout
	Writing, editing, and proofreading content to ensure it is free of errors and maximizes SEO
	Creating engaging content for social media platforms that aligns with brand voice and marketing goals
	Coordinating with team members to create innovative content ideas
	Monitoring feedback and online conversations about the brand or products to inform content creation
	Keeping up-to-date with industry trends and popular content strategies
	Measuring the success of content using analytics tools and adjusting strategies based on the data.
	Creatingengaging and on-brand content for a variety of platforms from conception through to delivery.


Requirements and Skills


	Proven work experience as a Content Creator,or similar rolein the hospitality industry
	Portfolio of published articles
	Hands-on experience with Content Management Systems (e.g. WordPress)
	Excellent writing and editing skills in English
	An ability to fact-check long-form content pieces
	Time-management skills
	Detail-oriented content creator
	Ability to produce high-quality content for our business.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Assistant",
  "description": "Malteser International has been working in Nigeria since 2017. In Northeast Nigeria and more specifically in Borno State, MI is currently active in the WASH sector for improving the lives of both the IDP population as well as the host communities. The WASH activities focus on improved access to safe water, sanitation and hygiene. They include NFI distribution, solarization of boreholes, rehabilitation of hand pumps, construction of latrines and hygiene promotion. Malteser International thereby applies a participatory, gender sensitive and inclusive approach.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Logistics Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Katsina

Responsibilities


	Provide overall support for logistics management at the Katsina location
	Assist in implementing procurement and logistics procedures in line with organizational and donor regulations
	Support preparation and processing of procurement documents, including bids and purchase requests
	Follow up on Goods Received Notes (GRN) and Job Completion Certificates from program teams
	Maintain proper filing systems and documentation for all logistics and procurement activities
	Manage warehouse/store operations and ensure regular asset tagging and inventory updates
	Coordinate vehicle requests, travel arrangements, and other logistical needs as required by the supervisor


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Logistics and Procurement, Business Administration, and or any related field of discipline
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant experience, preferably in an NGO or development sector
	Basic knowledge of stock counts, assets, inventories, and fleet management
	Individuals from Katsina State are encouraged to apply


What we offer


	Start Date: This role is intended for an upcoming project, subject to funding
	Duty Station: Katsina, Katsina State, Nigeria
	Duration of contract: 10 months
	Salary expectation: Salary according to the MI salary scale
	Other benefits: Group Life Insurance Premium, Health Insurance Package (Single/Family plan), 13th Month pay
	Annual leave + Compensation Leave + Academic Leave + Wedding Leave + Compassionate Leave - applicable
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-07",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Malteser International"
    
  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Logistics Assistant Job at Malteser International




        
    

        
        Malteser International has been working in Nigeria since 2017. In Northeast Nigeria and more specifically in Borno State, MI is currently active in the WASH sector for improving the lives of both the IDP population as well as the host communities. The WASH activities focus on improved access to safe water, sanitation and hygiene. They include NFI distribution, solarization of boreholes, rehabilitation of hand pumps, construction of latrines and hygiene promotion. Malteser International thereby applies a participatory, gender sensitive and inclusive approach.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Assistant

Job Location: Katsina
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Responsibilities


	Provide overall support for logistics management at the Katsina location
	Assist in implementing procurement and logistics procedures in line with organizational and donor regulations
	Support preparation and processing of procurement documents, including bids and purchase requests
	Follow up on Goods Received Notes (GRN) and Job Completion Certificates from program teams
	Maintain proper filing systems and documentation for all logistics and procurement activities
	Manage warehouse/store operations and ensure regular asset tagging and inventory updates
	Coordinate vehicle requests, travel arrangements, and other logistical needs as required by the supervisor


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Logistics and Procurement, Business Administration, and or any related field of discipline
	2–3 years of relevant experience, preferably in an NGO or development sector
	Basic knowledge of stock counts, assets, inventories, and fleet management
	Individuals from Katsina State are encouraged to apply


What we offer


	Start Date: This role is intended for an upcoming project, subject to funding
	Duty Station: Katsina, Katsina State, Nigeria
	Duration of contract: 10 months
	Salary expectation: Salary according to the MI salary scale
	Other benefits: Group Life Insurance Premium, Health Insurance Package (Single/Family plan), 13th Month pay
	Annual leave + Compensation Leave + Academic Leave + Wedding Leave + Compassionate Leave - applicable
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Head of Sales and Marketing Job at StarList Consulting




        
    

        
        StarList Consulting is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Sales and Marketing

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Working days: Mon-Sat

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are a rapidly growing premium hair brand with a strong footprint in Nigeria, the UK, the US, and Canada.
	As we expand, we are transitioning from founder-led sales to a structured, system-driven growth approach. We’re looking for a Head of Sales & Marketing to spearhead this transformation.
	This is not a maintenance role—you will design systems, close gaps, lead a high-performing team, and take full ownership of driving revenue growth.


What You’ll Do
Drive Revenue Growth:


	Own monthly and quarterly sales targets
	Build and manage a structured sales pipeline (lead → follow-up → close → repeat)
	Identify and fix revenue leakages across the funnel


Build & Optimize Sales Systems:


	Implement CRM and sales tracking systems
	Create dashboards to monitor performance (conversion, CAC, ROAS, etc.)
	Improve website conversion and customer journey


Expand Sales Channels:


	Grow channels beyond Instagram (WhatsApp, TikTok, website, email)
	Launch and scale B2B partnerships (salons, resellers, distributors)
	Develop multi-channel strategies to reduce dependency on a single platform


Lead the Commercial Team:


	Manage Sales, Digital Marketing, and Customer Service teams
	Set weekly targets and performance reviews
	Build scripts, playbooks, and scalable processes.


Own Marketing Strategy:


	Plan and allocate marketing budgets across Meta, TikTok, Google
	Ensure all campaigns are tied to revenue outcomes
	Monitor and optimize ROAS and acquisition costs


Improve Customer Retention:


	Build repeat purchase systems (email, WhatsApp, loyalty flows)
	Turn customer feedback into growth opportunities


What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days):


	CRM and sales pipeline fully implemented
	WhatsApp and new sales channels activated
	Website conversion rate improved to 2.5%+
	Measurable improvement in ROAS (minimum 3x)
	B2B pipeline actively generating opportunities
	Weekly performance dashboards running consistently.


Requirements
Experience:


	5+ years in sales/marketing (with leadership experience)
	Proven track record of hitting revenue targets
	Experience managing digital marketing (Meta, TikTok, Google)
	Background in e-commerce, beauty, fashion, or FMCG preferred
	Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
	Revenue-focused and results-driven
	Able to build systems from scratch
	Confident making data-backed decisions
	Comfortable working in a fast-paced, growing business.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 / month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (PDF), a short case study: a sales/marketing problem you solved + results, a brief answer (max 300 words) and Which sales bottleneck would you fix first—and why to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance and Admin Assistant",
  "description": "Malteser International has been working in Nigeria since 2017. In Northeast Nigeria and more specifically in Borno State, MI is currently active in the WASH sector for improving the lives of both the IDP population as well as the host communities. The WASH activities focus on improved access to safe water, sanitation and hygiene. They include NFI distribution, solarization of boreholes, rehabilitation of hand pumps, construction of latrines and hygiene promotion. Malteser International thereby applies a participatory, gender sensitive and inclusive approach.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Finance and Admin Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Katsina
Duration of contract: 10 months
Start Date: This role is intended for an upcoming project, subject to funding

Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records, including vouchers, receipts, and supporting documentation
	Prepare and process payment vouchers while ensuring compliance with financial policies and donor requirements
	Support cash and bank transactions, including petty cash management, reconciliations, and cash counts
	Assist in the preparation of monthly financial reports and proper filing/archiving of financial documents
	Provide general administrative support, including correspondence management, filing systems, and office documentation
	Support procurement and logistics processes, including vendor management, supply tracking, and documentation of goods/services
	Ensure compliance with internal controls and support audit processes while maintaining confidentiality of all financial and administrative information.


Qualification


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant experience, preferably in an NGO or development sector
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles and financial procedures
	Experience in administrative support roles is a major priority
	Individuals from Katsina State are highly encouraged to apply


What We Offer


	Salary expectation: Salary according to the MI salary scale
	Other benefits: Group Life Insurance Premium, Health Insurance Package (Single/Family plan), 13th Month pay
	Annual leave + Compensation Leave + Academic Leave + Wedding Leave + Compassionate Leave - applicable

Finance and Admin Assistant Job at Malteser International




        
    

        
        Malteser International has been working in Nigeria since 2017. In Northeast Nigeria and more specifically in Borno State, MI is currently active in the WASH sector for improving the lives of both the IDP population as well as the host communities. The WASH activities focus on improved access to safe water, sanitation and hygiene. They include NFI distribution, solarization of boreholes, rehabilitation of hand pumps, construction of latrines and hygiene promotion. Malteser International thereby applies a participatory, gender sensitive and inclusive approach.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance and Admin Assistant
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Katsina
Duration of contract: 10 months
Start Date: This role is intended for an upcoming project, subject to funding

Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records, including vouchers, receipts, and supporting documentation
	Prepare and process payment vouchers while ensuring compliance with financial policies and donor requirements
	Support cash and bank transactions, including petty cash management, reconciliations, and cash counts
	Assist in the preparation of monthly financial reports and proper filing/archiving of financial documents
	Provide general administrative support, including correspondence management, filing systems, and office documentation
	Support procurement and logistics processes, including vendor management, supply tracking, and documentation of goods/services
	Ensure compliance with internal controls and support audit processes while maintaining confidentiality of all financial and administrative information.


Qualification


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field
	2–3 years of relevant experience, preferably in an NGO or development sector
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles and financial procedures
	Experience in administrative support roles is a major priority
	Individuals from Katsina State are highly encouraged to apply


What We Offer


	Salary expectation: Salary according to the MI salary scale
	Other benefits: Group Life Insurance Premium, Health Insurance Package (Single/Family plan), 13th Month pay
	Annual leave + Compensation Leave + Academic Leave + Wedding Leave + Compassionate Leave - applicable
    

    

        Click here to Apply
